name: pydft
documentation:
# PyDFT

[![build](https://github.com/ifilot/pydft/actions/workflows/build_pypi.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ifilot/pydft/actions/workflows/build_pypi.yml)
[![build](https://github.com/ifilot/pydft/actions/workflows/build_conda.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ifilot/pydft/actions/workflows/build_conda.yml)
[![Anaconda-Server Badge](https://anaconda.org/ifilot/pydft/badges/version.svg)](https://anaconda.org/ifilot/pydft)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pydft?color=green)](https://pypi.org/project/pydft/)
[![License: GPL v3](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-GPLv3-blue.svg)](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0)

> [!NOTE]  
> Python based Density Functional Theory code for educational purposes. The
> documentation of PyDFT can be found [here](https://pydft.imc-tue.nl/).

## Purpose

PyDFT is a pure-Python package for performing localized-orbital DFT calculations
using Gaussian Type Orbitals. PyDFT currently supports LDA and PBE
exchange-correlation functionals. The purpose of PyDFT is mainly to serve as an
educational tool to explain the inner workings of a DFT calculation. This
program is not intended for professional calculations. It is not particularly
fast nor offers a lot of features that more mature open-source of commercial
packages offer. It **does** offer a unique insight into a working code and a
considerable effort was made in documenting everything.

> [!TIP]  
> Interested in other **education** quantum chemical codes? Have a look at the
> packages below.
> * [PyQInt](https://github.com/ifilot/pyqint) is a hybrid C++/Python (Cython)
>   code for performing Hartree-Fock calculations. This code contains many
>   relevant features, such a geometry optimization, orbital localization and
>   crystal orbital hamilton population analysis.
> * [HFCXX](https://github.com/ifilot/hfcxx) is a full C++ code for performing
>   Hartree-Fock calculations.
> * [DFTCXX](https://github.com/ifilot/dftcxx) is a full C++ code for performing
>   Density Functional Theory Calculations.

## Features

### Numerical integration using Becke grids

Electron-electron interaction terms (both classical as well as
exchange-correlation) are performed by means of extensive numerical integration
schemes performed over so-called Becke grids. Utilizing these grids, molecular
integrals are decomposed into series of weighted atomic integrals.

![Becke grids](img/becke-grid.png)

### Molecular orbital visualization

PyDFT can be readily used alongside [matplotlib](https://matplotlib.org/stable/)
to make figures of molecular orbitals or density fields.

![Molecular orbitals of CO](img/mo_co.png)

### Extensive output

Internal matrices, e.g. overlap or Hamiltonian matrix, are exposed to the user
and can be readily visualized using specific matrix visualization routines.

![Matrices](img/matrices.png)

## Installation

This code depends on a few other packages. To install this code and its
dependencies, run the following one-liner from Anaconda prompt

```bash
conda install -c ifilot pydft pyqint pylebedev pytessel
```

## Usage

### Check the current version

```python
import pydft
print(pydft.__version__)
```

### Performing a simple calculation

```python
from pydft import MoleculeBuilder, DFT

CO = MoleculeBuilder().get_molecule("CO")
dft = DFT(CO, basis='sto3g', verbose=True)
en = dft.scf(1e-4)
print("Total electronic energy: %f Ht" % en)
```

## Community guidelines

* Contributions to PyDFT are always welcome and appreciated. Before doing so,
  please first read the [CONTRIBUTING](CONTRIBUTING.md) guide.
* For reporting issues or problems with the software, you are kindly invited to
  to open a [new issue with the bug
  label](https://github.com/ifilot/pydft/issues/new?labels=bug).
* If you seek support in using PyDFT, please [open an issue with the
  question](https://github.com/ifilot/pydft/issues/new?labels=question) label.
* If you wish to contact the developers, please send an e-mail to ivo@ivofilot.nl.

## License

Unless otherwise stated, all code in this repository is provided under the GNU
General Public License version 3.

methods.name: pydft.xcfunctionals.Functionals.__pbe_x

documentation: PBE exchange derivative for spin-unpolarized density using reparametrization

methods.name: pydft.xcfunctionals.Functionals.__pbe_x_deriv_numerical

methods.name: pydft.xcfunctionals.Functionals.__pbe_c
documentation: Reparametrization of the PBE correlation functional for spin-unpolarized density

methods.name: pydft.xcfunctionals.Functionals.__pbe_c_deriv
documentation: PBE correlation derivative using numerical approximation

Class holding all exchange-correlation functionals

Note that all the functions ending in _deriv are the derivative
of the energy with respect to rho.

To calculate the potential, nu, one has to calculate
deltaf/deltarho = df/drho * rho + f

modules.name: pydft.spherical_harmonics
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: pydft.spherical_harmonics.spherical_harmonic
documentation:
Calculate value of spherical harmonic function

l:     angular quantum number
m:     magnetic quantum number
theta: azimuthal angle in radians
phi:   polar angle in radians

functions.name: pydft.spherical_harmonics.spherical_harmonic_cart
documentation:
Calculate the value of the spherical harmonic depending on the position
p in Cartesian coordinates. This function assumes that the position p
lies on the unit sphere

l:    angular quantum number
m:    magnetic quantum number
p:    position three-vector on the unit sphere

* This function should only be used after htpot has been generated

* This function should only be used for educational purposes

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.perform_spherical_harmonic_expansion
documentation:
Calculate the expansion coefficients using a basis set composed of
spherical harmonics given a atomic orbital

The expansion is performed over the function f which needs to be a matrix
of Nk x Nj elements where Nk is the number of radial points and Nj is
the number of angular points.

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.get_bragg_slater_radius
documentation:
Get the Bragg-Slater radius

Returns
-------
float
    Bragg-Slater radius

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.__calculate_density_coefficients
documentation:
Calculate the expansion coefficients using a basis set composed of
spherical harmonics given a atomic orbital

Note that we explicitly multiply with the Becke weights so that we
only integrate over the electron density assigned for this fuzzy cell.
That means that in any back-transformation, we should explicitly not
multiply with the Becke weights

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.__calculate_hartree_potential_coefficients
documentation: Calculate the Hartree potential using the finite difference approximation

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.calculate_nuclear_attraction
documentation: Calculate the nuclear attraction given the electron density

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.calculate_coulomb_energy
documentation:
Calculate the electron-electron repulsion for this atomic grid

This function is only for educational purposes, for any evaluation
of the coulomb energy, extensive interpolation over all the atomic
grids is necessary

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.calculate_coulomb_energy_interpolation
documentation:
Calculate the electron-electron repulsion for this atomic grid
using cubic spline interpolation

This function is only for educational purposes, for any evaluation
of the coulomb energy, extensive interpolation over all the atomic
grids is necessary

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.__build_weight_grid
documentation:
Calculates the weight factors on the grid and also builds the list
of coordinates of the grid points

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.__load_lebedev_coefficients
documentation:
Load Lebedev coefficients from data file and store these as a class
variable in a dictionary. Each set of angular points is stored as
a Nx4 matrix wherein the first three per row correspond to the
position on the unit sphere and the last row to the weight as used
in the Lebedev integration.

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.__build_chebychev_grid
documentation: Build Chebychev grid of concentric spheres

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.__build_ylm
documentation: Calculate the value for spherical harmonics at the angular points

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.__build_finite_difference_matrix
documentation:
Construct the finite difference matrix to solve for Ulm

The discretization points are automatically generated using the
'__calculate_fd_coeff' function. The user can specify the number of
grid points used via npts. The script always constructs a
npts-diagonal matrix, e.g. a heptadiagonal matrix when n=7 or a
pentadiagonal matrix when n-5.

r : vector of distances
rm: half of the Bragg-Slater radius
nrpts: number of stencil points

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.__calculate_fd_coeff
documentation:
Calculate finite difference coefficients

x: sampling points
k: derivative order

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.__set_bragg_slater_radius
documentation: Set the Bragg-Slater radius for the atom

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.__d2zdr2
documentation: Calculate second derivative of z-grid towards (regular) r-grid

methods.name: pydft.atomicgrid.AtomicGrid.__dzdrsq
documentation: Calculate square of first derivative of z-grid towards (regular) r-grid

modules.name: pydft.dft
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.__init__
documentation:
Constructs the DFT class

# Parameters

mol

: molecule

basis

: basis set, can be either a string or a list of cgf objects, by default 'sto3g'

functional

: exchange-correlation function, valid options are {code}`swn5`
  and {code}`pbe`, by default 'svwn5'

nshells

: number of radial shells, by default 32

nangpts

: number of angular sampling points, by default 110

lmax

: maximum value of l in the spherical harmonic expansion, by default 8

fdpts: int, optional

: number of grid point in finite difference scheme, by default 7

normalize: whether to perform intermediary normalization of the electron

: density

verbose

: whether to provide verbose output, by default False

parallel

: whether to use multiprocessing features, by default False

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.get_data
documentation:
Get relevant memory objects, only valid after successful SCF calculation

# Returns

dict

: Dictionary containing relevant memory objects (see below)

# Notes

The dictionary contains the following elements:

- {code}`S`: overlap matrix
- {code}`T`: kinetic energy matrix
- {code}`V`: nuclear attraction matrix
- {code}`C`: coefficient matrix
- {code}`J`: Hartree matrix
- {code}`P`: density matrix
- {code}`XC`: exchange-correlation matrix
- {code}`F`: Fock-matrix
- {code}`Exc`: exchange-correlation energy
- {code}`Ex`: exchange energy
- {code}`Ec`: correlation energy
- {code}`energies`: all energies
- {code}`energy`: last energy
- {code}`orbc`: coeffient matrix (duplicate)
- {code}`orbe`: molecular orbital eigenvalues
- {code}`enucrep`: electrostatic repulsion of the nuclei
- {code}`timedata`: computation time for various parts of the calculation

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.get_molgrid_copy
documentation:
Get a copy of the underlying molgrid object

Returns:

: MolecularGrid: copy of the {class}`MolecularGrid` object

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.get_density_at_points

Get the electron density at provided points

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.get_gradient_at_points
documentation:
Get the electron density gradient at provided points

# Parameters

spoints

: Set of sampling points ($X \times 3$ array)

# Returns

ndarray

: Electron density gradient vector field ($N \times 3$ array)

documentation:
Perform the self-consistent field procedure

Parameters
----------
tol : float, optional
    electronic convergence criterion, by default 1e-5

Returns
-------
float
    total electronic energy (in Hartrees)

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.get_construction_times
documentation:
Return construct times dictionary from MolecularGrid to get insights
into the time to construct particular properties

Returns

dict
    dictionary of construction times for the MolecularGrid objects

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.__iterate
documentation: Perform single-step iteration

documentation:
Construct the bare classes and matrices necessary to start the
self-consistent-field procedure

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.__calculate_J
documentation:
Calculate the coulombic interaction matrix using the
molecular grid

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.__calculate_P
documentation: Calculate density matrix from current coefficient matrix

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.__calculate_XC
documentation:
Calculate the exchange-correlation matrix and the
exchange-correlation energy

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.__calculate_diis_coefficients
documentation: Calculate the DIIS coefficients

methods.name: pydft.dft.DFT.__extrapolate_fock_from_diis_coefficients

modules.name: pydft.moleculargrid
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.__init__
documentation:
Construct MolecularGrid

Parameters
----------
atoms : list
    list of atoms and their charges
cgfs : list[pq.cgf]
    list of contracted Gaussian functions (basis set)
nshells : int, optional
    number of radial shells, by default 32
nangpts : int, optional
    number of angular sampling points per shell, by default 110
lmax : int, optional
    maximum value for l for projection of spherical harmonics, by default 8
fdpts: int, optional
    number of grid point in finite difference scheme, by default 7
functional : str, optional
    exchange-correlation functional, by default 'svwn5'
parallel : bool, optional
     whether to use multiprocessing features, by default False

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.initialize
documentation:
Initialize the molecular grid

This is a relatively expensive procedure and thus the user can
delay the initialization of the molecular grid until.

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.build_density
documentation:
Build the electron density from the density matrix

Parameters
----------
P : np.ndarray
    density matrix
normalize : bool, optional
    whether to normalize the density matrix based on the total number
    of electrons, by default True

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_becke_weights
documentation:
Get the Becke coefficients

# Returns

np.array

: $(N \times G \times 3)$ array with $N$ number of atoms
  and $G$ number of grid points

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_grid_coordinates
documentation:
Get the grid coordinates

# Returns

list[np.array]

: list over atoms with for each atom a $(G \times 1)$ array of
  electron densities where $G$ is the number of grid points
  per atom

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_hartree_potential
documentation:
Get the Hartree potential at each grid cell

# Returns

np.array

: $(G \times 1)$ array with $G$ number of molecular
  grid points

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_densities
documentation:
Get the densities at the grid points

# Returns

list[np.array]

: list over atoms with for each atom a $(G \times 1)$ array of
  electron densities where $G$ is the number of grid points
  per atom

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_gradients
documentation:
Get the gradient magnitude of the electron density field

# Returns

list[np.array]

: list over atoms with for each atom a $(G \times 1)$ array of
  electron gradient magnitudes where $G$ is the number of grid
  points per atom

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_rho_lm_atoms
documentation:
Get the electron density projected onto spherical harmonics per atom

# Returns

np.array

: $\rho_{n,lm}$ where $n$ loops over the atoms and the
  pairs $lm$ over the spherical harmonics

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_atomic_grid
documentation:
Get an atomic grid of atom $i$

# Parameters

i

: atom index

# Returns

AtomicGrid

: {class}`pydft.AtomicGrid` of atom $i$

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.count_electrons
documentation:
Count number of electrons in the system

Returns
-------
float
    Total number of electrons

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.count_electrons_from_rho_lm
documentation:
Count number of electrons from the spherical harmonic expansion; this
function mainly acts to verify that the spherical harmonic expansion
works correctly

Returns
-------
float
    Total number of electrons

Notes
-----
The Becke weights are already used when generating the density
coefficients and hence for the back-transformation, we should *not*
multiply with the Becke weights

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.calculate_dfa_nuclear_attraction_local
documentation:
Get density functional approximation for the nuclear attraction energy
using only the local potential for each atomic grid and ignoring the
influence of atomic attraction due to other atoms

Returns
-------
float
    Approximate nuclear attraction energy (Hartree)

Notes
-----
This function will of course not give an accurate result and is only
meant for educational purposes

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.calculate_dfa_nuclear_attraction_full
documentation:
Get density functional approximation for the nuclear attraction energy

Returns
-------
float
    Total nuclear attraction energy (Hartree)

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.calculate_dfa_coulomb
documentation:
Get density functional approximation for the coulombic repulsion 
between electrons

Returns
-------
float
    Total coulombic repulsion (Hartree)

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.calculate_dfa_coulomb_no_interpolation
documentation:
Calculate the Coulombic self-repulsion in the limit of the sum of atomic centers,
without any interpolation of the contribution of the other atoms

Returns
-------
float
    Approximate coulombic repulsion (Hartree)

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.calculate_coulombic_matrix
documentation:
Build the coulomb matrix $\mathbf{J}$

# Returns

np.array

: Coulomb matrix $\mathbf{J}$

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.calculate_dfa_kinetic
documentation:
Get density functional approximation for the kinetic energy

Returns
-------
float
    Total kinetic energy (Hartree)

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.calculate_dfa_exchange
documentation:
Get density functional approximation for the exchange energy

Returns
-------
float
    Total exchange energy (Hartree)

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.calculate_exchange
documentation:
Build the exchange matrix and give the exchange energy for the molecule

# Returns

np.ndarray,float

: Exchange matrix $\mathbf{X}$ and exchange energy

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.calculate_correlation
documentation:
Build the correlation matrix and give the correlation energy for the molecule

Returns
-------
np.ndarray,float
    Correlation matrix and exchange energy

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_density_at_points
documentation:
Calculate the electron density at the points as given by spoints
using density matrix P

# Parameters

spoints

: Array of grid points $(N \times 3)$ with $N$ the number
  of grid points

P

: Density matrix $(K \times K)$ with $K$ the number of
  basis functions

# Returns

np.array

: Electron density specified at grid points $(N \times 1)$
  with $N$ the number of grid points

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_amplitude_at_points
documentation:
Calculate the wave function amplitude at the points as given by
spoints using solution vector c

# Parameters

spoints

: Array of grid points $(N \times 3)$ with $N$ the number
  of grid points

c

: Coefficient vector $\vec{c}$ of a single molecular orbital

# Returns

np.array

: Electron density specified at grid points $(N \times 1)$
  with $N$ the number of grid points

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_gradient_at_points
documentation:
Calculate the gradient of the electron density at the points as
given by spoints using density matrix $\mathbf{P}$

# Parameters

spoints

: Array of grid points $(N \times 3)$ with $N$ the number
  of grid points

P

: Density matrix $(K \times K)$ with $K$ the number of
  basis functions

# Returns

np.array

: Electron density gradients at grid points $(N \times 3)$
  with $N$ the number of grid points

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.calculate_coulomb_potential_at_points
documentation:
Collect the spherical harmonic expansion coefficients of the hartree
potential for all the atoms at the grid points

# Parameters

pts

: Array of grid points $(N \times 3)$ with $N$ the number
  of grid points

# Returns

np.array

: Coulomb potential at grid points $(\left[l_{\textrm{max}}+1\right]^{2} \times N)$
  with $N$ the number of grid points

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_exchange_potential_at_points
documentation:
Get the exchange potential at points pts

# Parameters

pts

: Array of grid points $(N \times 3)$ with $N$ the number
  of grid points

P

: Density matrix $(K \times K)$ with $K$ the number of
  basis functions

# Returns

np.ndarray

: Exchange potential $\nu_{x}$ at grid points $(N \times 1)$
  with $N$ the number of grid points

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_correlation_potential_at_points
documentation:
Get the correlation potential at points pts

# Parameters

pts

: Array of grid points $(N \times 3)$ with $N$ the number
  of grid points

P

: Density matrix $(K \times K)$ with $K$ the number of
  basis functions

# Returns

np.ndarray

: Correlation potential $\nu_{c}$ at grid points $(N \times 1)$
  with $N$ the number of grid points

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.get_spherical_harmonic_expansion_of_amplitude
documentation:
Get the spherical harmonic expansion representation of a wavefunction
amplitude using solution vector c

# Parameters

c

: Wave function expansion coefficient

radial_factor

: whether to multiply result by $r^{2}$, by default False

# Returns

list[np.ndarray]

: List of spherical harmonic expansions per atom stored as
  $(N_{r} \times N_{lm})$ arrays where $N_{r}$ is the
  number of radial points and $N_{lm}$ the set of spherical
  harmonics used in the expansion.

methods.name: pydft.moleculargrid.MolecularGrid.calculate_weights_at_points
documentation:
Custom function that (re-)calculates weights at given points

# Parameters

points

: $N \times 3$ array of grid points

k

: smoothing value, by default 3

# Returns

np.ndarray

: $N_{a} \times N$ array with $N_{a}$ the number of
  atoms and $N$ the number of grid points
